title: carya ovalis subsp. australis
intro: Carya ovalis subsp. australis, also known as the Hill Walnut or Ironwood, is a subspecies of Carya ovalis, a deciduous tree native to eastern Australia. The medicinal properties of Carya ovalis subsp. australis include its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids in its bark and leaves. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to support cardiovascular health, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant properties, making it a potential remedy for various health conditions. In herbal preparations, the bark and leaves of Carya ovalis subsp. australis can be used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
